# 3D AI Pricing & Credits Comparison (2026): Sloyd vs Meshy vs Tripo vs CSM vs Hyper3D

Avi Latner

September 4, 2025

• 

3

min read

Comparing prices between 3D AI model generators is cumbersome: Every system grants tokens for a different price. We did the hard work for you, and reading this will save you a ton of money!

As AI-powered 3D model creation takes center stage, developers and designers face an increasingly crowded marketplace. Prices fluctuate significantly, and features vary widely. We've broken down the costs so you can make an informed choice without getting lost in the details.

## **The Ultimate 3D AI Price Comparison**

‍

Platform | Plan used | Monthly price | Monthly credits | Credits / textured model | Models / month | Effective $ / model | 100 models / mo | 500 models / mo | 1,000 models / mo  
---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---  
Sloyd | Plus (monthly) | $15.00 | Unlimited (no credits) | N/A | Unlimited | $0.015 _(assumes 1,000 models/mo)_ | $15.00 | $15.00 | $15.00  
Meshy | Pro ($20/month) | $20.00 | 1,000 | 20 _(10 model + 10 texture)_ | 50 | $0.40 | $40.00 | $200.00 | $400.00  
Tripo | Professional ($15.9/month) | $15.90 | 3,000 | 40 _(HD-texture image-based generation)_ | 75 | $0.212 | $21.20 | $106.00 | $212.00  
**Notes:**

  * **Meshy:** “Textured model” modeled as Model Stage (10 credits) + Texture Stage (10 credits) = 20 credits.
  * **Tripo:** “Textured model” modeled as HD-texture image-based generation = 40 credits (style and quad can add +5 each).
  * **Scenario costs (100/500/1,000):** computed as _Effective $/model × model count_. If you require whole-month subscriptions only, you’d round up months to cover the volume.
  * **Sloyd Effective $/model:** shown at 1,000 models/month purely to make a numeric comparison; flat monthly cost stays $15.

  
  
‍

## **Why Do Prices Vary So Much?**

Much like the fashion industry, where branding dramatically influences price, the 3D AI market sees stark pricing contrasts—but here branding doesn't play a significant role. So, what drives this disparity?

## **Different Features for Different Needs**

While every service benchmarked offers core functionalities like text-to-3D and image-to-3D model generation with textures, each has unique strengths:

  * Meshy: Recently added character animations.
  * Tripo: Offers 3D printing and ship modes.
  * CSM: Facilitates breaking objects into separable parts.
  * Sloyd: Visual editing of parametric generators.
  * 3D studio AI: Text-to-texture creation.

## Making Sense of Credits and Tiers

‍

Most services complicate direct comparisons due to different tier structures, each including varying numbers of credits. Credits act as virtual currency, and their value varies significantly between providers.

Consider a use-case indie game developer creating a level with around 300 unique models—characters, weapons, loot boxes, and environmental art. Realistically, this would take about 1,000 model generations considering iterations and variations. To simplify, we take the basic tier price for each platform and the credits it would cost to generate one textured model from an image. 

## **The Bottom Line**

In 2026, navigating 3D AI creation tools is more affordable than ever—provided you pick wisely.

If you’re just trying it out, or you know you only need a few models, then the monthly price difference between providers is not that drastic. Still with a 2X difference between the most affordable platform to the most expensive one, price is an important consideration.

For users who need large volumes price becomes a critical consideration with a whooping 33X between the most affordable and most expensive platform. Sloyd stands out as the clear winner in terms of sheer cost efficiency, especially valuable for indie developers aiming for scalable and affordable creativity.

Sloyd is the only provider that grants unlimited generations without tokens, it's the top choice for a flourishing creative project without breaking the bank.
